skill.name: azure-keyvault-secrets-ts
skill.description: Manage secrets using Azure Key Vault Secrets SDK for JavaScript (@azure/keyvault-secrets). Use when storing and retrieving application secrets or configuration values.
skill.summary: Securely manage secrets and cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault using the official TypeScript SDK with industry-standard encryption.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged markdown documentation code blocks as executable code. The skill uses Azure SDK patterns correctly with DefaultAzureCredential, environment-based configuration, and industry-standard cryptographic algorithms (RSA-OAEP, RS256, P-256). No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Key Vault secrets securely
content.value_statement: Storing application secrets in code creates security risks. This skill enables secure secret management using Azure Key Vault with proper authentication and encryption.
actual_capabilities: Create, retrieve, update, and delete secrets in Azure Key Vault
Manage cryptographic keys with RSA, EC, and rotation policies
Perform encrypt, decrypt, sign, and verify operations
Backup and restore secrets and keys across vaults
List secrets and key versions with pagination support
Handle soft-delete and purge operations for recovery
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Key Vault instance
Node.js only - browser environments not supported
DefaultAzureCredential requires proper Azure identity configuration
Network access to Azure Key Vault endpoint required

use_cases.title: Secure Application Configuration
use_cases.description: Store database connection strings, API keys, and service credentials in Key Vault instead of environment variables or config files.

use_cases.title: Cryptographic Key Management
use_cases.description: Generate and manage RSA or elliptic curve keys for encryption and digital signatures with automatic rotation policies.

use_cases.title: Secrets Rotation Automation
use_cases.description: Implement automated secret rotation with expiration dates and access policies to reduce credential exposure risk.

prompt_templates.title: Get a secret from Key Vault
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the secret named 'DatabaseConnectionString' from Azure Key Vault and log its value.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Basic secret retrieval for application startup

prompt_templates.title: Store a new secret with attributes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new secret called 'ApiKey' with the value 'xyz123', set it to enabled, add a contentType of 'text/plain', and tag it with environment=production.

prompt_templates.title: Encrypt and decrypt data with Key Vault
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the CryptographyClient to encrypt a message with RSA-OAEP algorithm, then decrypt it and verify the original content is preserved.

prompt_templates.title: Implement key rotation with policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an RSA key with 2048-bit size, configure a rotation policy to auto-rotate every 90 days with a 30-day pre-expiry action, and set up monitoring for rotation events.

output_examples.input: Get the secret 'MySecret' from Key Vault
output_examples.output: Secret retrieved successfully: MySecret (version: a1b2c3d4). Value length: 32 characters. Content type: application/json. Expires: 2025-12-31.

output_examples.input: List all secrets in the vault
output_examples.output: Found 5 secrets: DatabaseConnectionString, ApiKey, JwtSigningKey, SmtpPassword, StorageAccountKey. All secrets are enabled. 2 secrets expire within 30 days.
Key rotated successfully: EncryptionKey. New version: e5f6g7h8. Key type: RSA. Key size: 2048 bits. Rotation completed at 2026-02-25T00:22:36Z.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for seamless authentication across development and production environments
Enable soft-delete on production vaults to allow secret recovery within the retention period
Set expiration dates on secrets and configure automatic rotation policies for cryptographic keys
anti_patterns: Never hardcode secret values in source code or commit them to version control
Avoid using master keys or root credentials - implement least-privilege access with specific key operations
Do not disable soft-delete in production environments as it prevents recovery from accidental deletion

faq.question: What Azure permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Key Vault Secrets User role for secret operations, or Key Vault Crypto User for key operations. For management tasks like creating vaults, Key Vault Administrator role is required.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in browser-based applications?
faq.answer: No, the @azure/keyvault-secrets and @azure/keyvault-keys SDKs are designed for Node.js environments only. Browser applications should use Azure Key Vault Managed HSM with appropriate client-side libraries.

faq.question: How do I authenticate locally versus in Azure?
faq.answer: DefaultAzureCredential automatically handles both: it uses your Azure CLI login for local development and managed identities when running in Azure (App Service, Functions, VMs).

faq.question: What happens when a secret expires?
faq.answer: Expired secrets remain accessible by default unless the vault has expiration policies enforced. The enabled attribute controls whether a secret can be used - set enabled:false to disable before expiration.

faq.question: Can I restore a deleted secret to a different vault?
faq.answer: Yes, backup and restore operations are portable across vaults. Backup creates an encrypted blob that can be restored to any vault you have access to, including in different subscriptions.

faq.question: How are soft-delete and purge protection different?
faq.answer: Soft-delete moves deleted items to a recoverable state for 7-90 days. Purge protection prevents permanent deletion during the soft-delete retention period, protecting against malicious insiders.
